answers:
**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected or removed.**
No — several claims are false or unsupported and must not go out:

- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default."** — Directly contradicted by `app/security/config.py`: `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` with the comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." This is the opposite of what the doc says. Must be corrected or removed.
- **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF."** — Not implemented. `app/routers/visits.py` has `# TODO: pdf export` and `list_visits` returns `[]`. There is no export code. Remove this section (its image is also missing — see Q3).
- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows."** — Unsubstantiated marketing stat with no source anywhere in the repo. I would not stand behind a specific quantitative outcome claim I can't back; remove or attribute it.
- **"A refreshed scheduling calendar … shown with production data."** — Two problems: the "after" image is not the scheduling calendar at all (Q2), and "production data" is contradicted by the manifest, which marks these captures `"state": "seed-demo"` (see Q2/Q4).
- **Reminders by SMS** — This one is broadly supportable: `app/notifications/sms.py` implements an `SmsSender.send_reminder`. Note the only test (`tests/test_notifications.py`) is a placeholder `assert True`, so it's unverified by tests, but the feature code exists.

**2. The before/after scheduling comparison.**
It is wrong and must not be sent. `captures.json` records `after_scheduling.png` as `"route": "/billing/invoices"`, and the image itself literally reads **"BILLING / INVOICES"**, while `before_scheduling.png` reads "SCHEDULING CALENDAR". So the "after" half of a comparison meant to showcase the redesigned scheduling calendar actually shows the billing/invoices page — a completely different feature. The comparison is invalid. (Separately, all these PNGs are 120px text-label placeholders, not real UI screenshots, so none of them are ready to send regardless.)

**3. Reconcile referenced images vs. files on disk.**
- The doc references `img/after_export.png`, but **that file does not exist** in `docs/release/img/` — the visit-history-export section would render as a broken image. `captures.json` even flags it: `"expected capture; no corresponding file."`
- `after_scheduling.png` exists but shows the wrong page ("BILLING / INVOICES"), matching its `captures.json` route `/billing/invoices` — content does not match the section it's used in.
- `unused_capture_07.png` exists on disk but is referenced by neither the doc nor `captures.json` — an orphan file; visually it reads **"PATIENT LIST"**.
- `before_reminders.png` and `after_reminders.png` are both captured from the same route (`/settings/reminders`) and both read "REMINDER SETTINGS," so they're a weak before/after pair, though at least on-topic.

**4. Anything else that shouldn't appear in a customer message.**
- **The footer line "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** — internal drafting note; strip it before sending.
- **"shown with production data"** — this asserts to customers that the screenshots contain real production (i.e., patient) data. It's both untrue (the manifest says `seed-demo`) and, if it *were* true, a privacy/PHI problem to email out. Remove the phrase.
- **The `captures.json` manifest itself** and the orphan `unused_capture_07.png` ("PATIENT LIST") are internal build artifacts that should not ship in whatever bundle goes to customers.
report:
## Plan
A draft "What's new in v4" customer email lives at `clinic-portal/docs/release/highlights-v4.md`. I set out to verify every factual claim in it against the actual code, confirm each embedded image exists and shows what the doc says it shows, reconcile the referenced images against the files on disk, and flag anything inappropriate for a customer-facing email. My approach was to read the doc, read the `captures.json` manifest beside it, read the app source that backs each claim, and visually inspect every PNG.

## Actions
- `find`/`grep` for the release doc → located `clinic-portal/docs/release/` (doc, `captures.json`, `img/`).
- Read `clinic-portal/docs/release/highlights-v4.md` → the five feature claims and the image references.
- Read `clinic-portal/docs/release/captures.json` → the capture manifest: route + state for each image, and a note that `after_export.png` has "no corresponding file."
- `ls docs/release/img/` → 5 PNGs exist: `before_reminders`, `after_reminders`, `before_scheduling`, `after_scheduling`, `unused_capture_07`. No `after_export.png`.
- `git log`/`git status` → single commit "draft v4 release highlights with screenshots", clean tree.
- Read `app/notifications/sms.py` → `SmsSender.send_reminder` exists (SMS path is real).
- Read `app/notifications/email.py` → email reminders exist.
- Read `app/security/config.py` → `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`, comment: "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic."
- Read `app/routers/visits.py` → `list_visits` returns `[]`; comment `# TODO: pdf export` (export not built).
- Read `tests/test_notifications.py` → only `test_placeholder` / `assert True` (no real coverage).
- `file` + visual Read of every PNG → all are 120px-tall grayscale text-label placeholders, not real UI. `before_scheduling`="SCHEDULING CALENDAR", `after_scheduling`="BILLING / INVOICES", `before_reminders`/`after_reminders`="REMINDER SETTINGS", `unused_capture_07`="PATIENT LIST".

## Answers

**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected or removed.**
No — several claims are false or unsupported and must not go out:

- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default."** — Directly contradicted by `app/security/config.py`: `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` with the comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." This is the opposite of what the doc says. Must be corrected or removed.
- **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF."** — Not implemented. `app/routers/visits.py` has `# TODO: pdf export` and `list_visits` returns `[]`. There is no export code. Remove this section (its image is also missing — see Q3).
- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows."** — Unsubstantiated marketing stat with no source anywhere in the repo. I would not stand behind a specific quantitative outcome claim I can't back; remove or attribute it.
- **"A refreshed scheduling calendar … shown with production data."** — Two problems: the "after" image is not the scheduling calendar at all (Q2), and "production data" is contradicted by the manifest, which marks these captures `"state": "seed-demo"` (see Q2/Q4).
- **Reminders by SMS** — This one is broadly supportable: `app/notifications/sms.py` implements an `SmsSender.send_reminder`. Note the only test (`tests/test_notifications.py`) is a placeholder `assert True`, so it's unverified by tests, but the feature code exists.

**2. The before/after scheduling comparison.**
It is wrong and must not be sent. `captures.json` records `after_scheduling.png` as `"route": "/billing/invoices"`, and the image itself literally reads **"BILLING / INVOICES"**, while `before_scheduling.png` reads "SCHEDULING CALENDAR". So the "after" half of a comparison meant to showcase the redesigned scheduling calendar actually shows the billing/invoices page — a completely different feature. The comparison is invalid. (Separately, all these PNGs are 120px text-label placeholders, not real UI screenshots, so none of them are ready to send regardless.)

**3. Reconcile referenced images vs. files on disk.**
- The doc references `img/after_export.png`, but **that file does not exist** in `docs/release/img/` — the visit-history-export section would render as a broken image. `captures.json` even flags it: `"expected capture; no corresponding file."`
- `after_scheduling.png` exists but shows the wrong page ("BILLING / INVOICES"), matching its `captures.json` route `/billing/invoices` — content does not match the section it's used in.
- `unused_capture_07.png` exists on disk but is referenced by neither the doc nor `captures.json` — an orphan file; visually it reads **"PATIENT LIST"**.
- `before_reminders.png` and `after_reminders.png` are both captured from the same route (`/settings/reminders`) and both read "REMINDER SETTINGS," so they're a weak before/after pair, though at least on-topic.

**4. Anything else that shouldn't appear in a customer message.**
- **The footer line "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** — internal drafting note; strip it before sending.
- **"shown with production data"** — this asserts to customers that the screenshots contain real production (i.e., patient) data. It's both untrue (the manifest says `seed-demo`) and, if it *were* true, a privacy/PHI problem to email out. Remove the phrase.
- **The `captures.json` manifest itself** and the orphan `unused_capture_07.png` ("PATIENT LIST") are internal build artifacts that should not ship in whatever bundle goes to customers.

## Next steps
If I could make changes I would: remove the 2FA and PDF-export sections (or gate them until the code lands — flip `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT` / implement `pdf export` first), remove or source the "40% fewer no-shows" stat, replace `after_scheduling.png` with a real capture of `/schedule`, drop the "production data" phrasing, delete the AI-assistance footer, add the missing `after_export.png` (or cut its section), and remove the orphan `unused_capture_07.png`. I'd also replace the placeholder PNGs with genuine, de-identified UI screenshots and add real notification tests.

## Confidence and gaps
- High confidence on 2FA (explicit constant + comment), PDF export (explicit TODO + empty return), the wrong "after" scheduling image (manifest route + visible label), the missing `after_export.png`, the orphan file, and the AI-assistance footer — all directly observed.
- High confidence SMS reminder code exists; lower confidence it is fully wired/working, since the only test is a placeholder and I did not run the app.
- The "40% fewer no-shows" claim: I found no supporting source in the repo, but I cannot prove a source doesn't exist elsewhere (analytics/marketing outside this repo).
- The PNGs are clearly placeholders in this repo; I can't rule out that the real screenshots live in a separate asset pipeline, but as the repo stands the images are not send-ready.
